title: Recover remote result chain for context slimming real sampling retry
objective: Handle the current server-side result-chain failure for task 20260410-context-slimming-real-sampling-retry-v1. First try to recover a normal terminal result from /api/v1/results. If the task remains running without a result, perform one controlled reset of the stale running task and one re-dispatch of the same real-sampling objective, then collect the final terminal result or the first repair blocker.

references.description: The most recent dispatch task that led to the current running-without-result state.
Structured result that incorrectly closed early while the remote executor was still running.

remote_task.expected_goal: Run one real worker sample and one real manager sample with slimmed prompt path; capture real prompt/token evidence; verify trusted broadcast/worker evidence/manager gate do not regress.

required_work.description: Read /api/v1/state and /api/v1/results using the current controller environment. Determine whether the remote task already has a terminal result.
If the result is already present in /api/v1/results, stop repair flow immediately and collect the result instead of resetting anything.
If the task is still running and /api/v1/results still has no matching terminal result, classify it as a result-chain recovery case rather than a task-content failure.
If the task remains running without a result, perform exactly one controlled POST /api/v1/admin/reset-running-task with a precise reason describing result-chain recovery.
After a confirmed reset, re-dispatch the same real-sampling objective exactly once. Do not create multiple duplicate remote tasks.
After one recovery cycle, either collect the remote terminal result or stop at the first concrete blocker with evidence.
Write detailed execution logs to the main-thread log folder and publish a structured terminal result with evidence and log paths.

do_not: Do not classify the current situation as a content/workflow regression before checking result-chain recovery.
Do not spam repeated resets or repeated redispatches. Allow at most one reset and one re-dispatch in this repair task.
Do not create multiple duplicate remote tasks for the same retry objective.
Do not expand this task into UI work, feature work, or new protocol changes.
acceptance: Either a matching terminal result is recovered from /api/v1/results, or a precise first repair blocker is produced after one controlled recovery cycle.
The result chain is treated as a server repair problem, not confused with workflow-content failure.
If reset is used, the reset response and re-dispatch response are recorded.
A structured terminal result exists with evidence paths and a log path.

deliverables.description: Main-thread log root for detailed logs.

working_principles: First prefer normal result recovery over resetting active work.
If accepted+runs but no result appears, handle it as a server repair flow.
Use one controlled reset and one single re-dispatch at most.
Keep the task narrow: result-chain recovery only.
